title: Cardiology Progress Note — Visit 2024-10-20
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 58-year-old male with a history of CAD, post-CABG in 2021, presents for evaluation of intermittent exertional chest tightness. The tightness occurs after walking 2 blocks, radiating to the left shoulder, and is relieved within 5 minutes of rest. Denies syncope, palpitations, or orthopnea.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: Temp 98.4F, HR 72 (regular), BP 138/84, RR 16, SpO2 97% on RA. General: Alert and oriented, in no acute distress. Cardiovascular: S1, S2 normal, soft S4 gallop heard at the apex, no murmurs. Pulses: 2+ symmetrically in upper extremities, 1+ dorsalis pedis bilaterally. Extremities: Mild 1+ bilateral ankle pitting edema.

ASSESSMENT: Exertional angina pectoris in a patient with established CAD and history of bypass surgery.

PLAN: Continue metoprolol succinate 50mg daily and atorvastatin 80mg daily. Add isosorbide mononitrate 30mg daily for anginal prevention. Schedule a nuclear myocardial perfusion stress test to evaluate for reversible ischemia. Return to clinic in 4 weeks.

title: Endocrinology Consult — Visit 2024-11-12
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 62-year-old female presents for management of type 2 diabetes mellitus and chronic kidney disease. Reports good adherence to her insulin regimen but has experienced occasional nocturnal hypoglycemia. Complains of mild bilateral foot numbness.

OBJECTIVE: BP 130/76, HR 68, RR 14. Labs: HbA1c is elevated at 7.9% (previous 8.4%). Serum creatinine is 1.45 mg/dL, with an eGFR of 52 mL/min/1.73m², consistent with Stage 3 CKD. Urinalysis shows microalbuminuria.

ASSESSMENT:
1. Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic nephropathy, currently suboptimally controlled.
2. Chronic kidney disease, Stage 3a.
3. Diabetic peripheral neuropathy.

PLAN: Decrease glargine insulin from 30 units to 26 units at bedtime to prevent hypoglycemia. Continue metformin 500mg BID. Initiate Jardiance (empagliflozin) 10mg daily for renal protection and glycemic control. Schedule follow-up labs (A1c, BMP) in 3 months. Refer to podiatry for a comprehensive diabetic foot exam.

title: Pulmonology Consult — Visit 2024-06-27
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 68-year-old male with a 40 pack-year smoking history presents with worsening shortness of breath and an increase in thick, yellow sputum production over the last 4 days. He has a known history of severe COPD (GOLD Stage III).

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: Temp 99.0F, HR 92, BP 128/82, RR 22, SpO2 89% on room air (RA). General: Alert, speaking in clipped sentences, using accessory muscles of respiration. Respiratory: Barrel chest noted. Auscultation reveals diffuse expiratory wheezing and coarse rales throughout both lung fields. Cardiac: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmurs.

ASSESSMENT: Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), likely triggered by bronchitis.

PLAN: Initiate a 5-day course of prednisone 40mg daily and azithromycin 250mg daily. Cautious oxygen supplementation to keep SpO2 between 90-92%. Increase frequency of Albuterol/Ipratropium (DuoNeb) inhalations to every 4 hours PRN. Patient instructed to go to the Emergency Department if respiratory distress worsens.

title: Neurology Progress Note — Visit 2024-03-02
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 42-year-old female presents with a history of intractable chronic migraine headaches with aura. She reports 12 headache days per month. The headaches are throbbing, unilateral, and accompanied by severe photophobia, phonophobia, and nausea.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals are normal. Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII are intact. Sensation, motor strength (5/5), and deep tendon reflexes are normal and symmetrical. Mild photophobia noted during the examination.

ASSESSMENT: Intractable chronic migraine with aura, poorly responsive to over-the-counter analgesics.

PLAN: Initiate prophylactic therapy with topiramate 25mg daily, titrating up to 50mg twice daily over the next 4 weeks. For acute abortive treatment, prescribe sumatriptan 50mg orally, to be taken at the onset of aura or headache (maximum 2 doses per 24 hours). Recommend keeping a detailed headache diary to track triggers.

title: Orthopedic Consult — Visit 2024-05-12
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 35-year-old male presents with right knee pain following a twisting injury during a soccer match 2 days ago. He reports hearing a pop, followed by immediate swelling and an inability to bear weight fully. Complains of the knee 'locking' and 'giving way'.

OBJECTIVE: Swelling and moderate joint effusion noted in the right knee. Range of motion is limited: flexion is restricted to 90 degrees due to pain. Tender to palpation along the medial joint line. Positive McMurray test and positive Lachman test.

ASSESSMENT: Acute right knee injury. Suspected tear of the medial meniscus and potential anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) tear.

PLAN: Obtain a right knee MRI to confirm ligamentous and meniscal pathology. Instructed on RICE protocol (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation). Provide crutches and a knee immobilizer for ambulation. Prescribe ibuprofen 600mg every 8 hours PRN for pain and inflammation.

title: Psychiatric Intake — Visit 2024-09-15
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 28-year-old female presents for psychiatric evaluation reporting persistent low mood, lack of energy, and generalized anxiety for the past 6 months. She reports difficulties concentrating at work and anhedonia. Denies suicidal ideation or auditory hallucinations.

OBJECTIVE: Mental Status Exam: Clean, casually dressed. Speech is slow and soft. Affect is flat and congruent with a depressed mood. Thought process is linear but restricted. PHQ-9 score is 18 (indicative of moderately severe depression) and GAD-7 score is 15 (indicative of severe anxiety).

ASSESSMENT: Moderate Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) and Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD).

PLAN: Initiate Lexapro (escitalopram) 10mg daily for depression and anxiety. Refer the patient to cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) for weekly psychotherapy sessions. Educate on potential side effects, including temporary nausea and sleep disturbances. Schedule a follow-up visit in 2 weeks to assess tolerability.

title: Gastroenterology Consult — Visit 2024-08-20
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 49-year-old female presents with a 3-month history of severe epigastric burning pain, which worsens after meals and when lying down. Reports frequent acid regurgitation and occasional dysphagia. Denies hematemesis, melena, or unexplained weight loss.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: stable. Abdomen is soft, symmetric, and non-distended, but with moderate tenderness to palpation in the epigastric region. No rebound tenderness, guarding, or organomegaly.

ASSESSMENT:
1. Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with possible esophagitis.
2. Suspected gastritis or peptic ulcer disease.

PLAN: Initiate Omeprazole 40mg daily, to be taken 30 minutes before the first meal. Advise dietary modifications: avoid caffeine, spicy foods, and eating within 3 hours of bedtime. Schedule an outpatient EGD (esophagogastroduodenoscopy) to evaluate the esophagus and gastric mucosa. Return to clinic in 4 weeks.

title: Pediatric Well-Check — Visit 2024-04-18
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 4-year-old female presents with her mother for a routine well-child check. Mother reports the child is eating well, sleeping 10 hours a night, and meeting all developmental milestones, including speaking in full sentences and riding a tricycle.

OBJECTIVE: Weight: 16.5 kg (60th percentile), Height: 102 cm (55th percentile). General: Well-developed, active, and cooperative. HEENT: Tympanic membranes clear, throat without erythema. Heart: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, no masses.

ASSESSMENT: Healthy 4-year-old child with normal physical development and age-appropriate milestones.

PLAN: Administer scheduled immunizations: DTaP, IPV, MMR, and Varicella vaccines. Provide guidance on safety, nutrition, and screen time limitations. Schedule the next routine well-child visit in 1 year.

title: Oncology Follow-Up — Visit 2024-05-02
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 54-year-old female with a history of Stage II hormone receptor-positive breast cancer, status post-lumpectomy and radiation in 2022, presents for a routine surveillance follow-up. She complains of mild hot flashes and generalized fatigue but denies bone pain, cough, or local breast changes.

OBJECTIVE: Physical Exam: Lumpectomy scar on the left breast is well-healed, with no palpable masses or skin changes. No contralateral breast masses, axillary lymphadenopathy, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Lungs are clear and abdomen is soft and non-tender.

ASSESSMENT: Stage II breast cancer, status post-therapy, with no clinical evidence of local recurrence or systemic metastasis.

PLAN: Continue adjuvant endocrine therapy with Anastrozole 1mg daily for a total course of 5 years. Manage hot flashes with lifestyle changes. Order a surveillance bilateral screening mammogram. Schedule follow-up clinic visit in 6 months.

title: Emergency Dept Discharge — Visit 2024-09-05
text:
SUBJECTIVE: A 29-year-old female presents to the Emergency Department complaining of high fever, chills, and severe right-sided flank pain for the last 24 hours. Reports dysuria, urinary frequency, and nausea.

OBJECTIVE: Vitals: Temp 101.8F, HR 104, BP 112/68, RR 18, SpO2 98% on RA. General: Ill-appearing, shivering. Abdomen: Soft, but with severe right costovertebral angle (CVA) tenderness. Labs: Urinalysis shows pyuria, hematuria, and is positive for nitrites and leukocyte esterase, consistent with acute urinary tract infection.

ASSESSMENT: Acute uncomplicated pyelonephritis.

PLAN: Administer 1g of IV Rocephin (ceftriaxone) in the ED. Discharge the patient on oral Ciprofloxacin 500mg BID for 7 days. Instructed to take acetaminophen 650mg every 6 hours PRN for fever. Advised to follow up with her primary care physician in 48 hours, and return immediately if vomiting or unable to tolerate oral fluids.
